author: y4rr
content:
Result: rewrote flex_attention to compose existing GPU-aware tensor ops instead of downloading Q/K/V to CPU. The new path is bmm_differentiable(Q, K^T) → mul(scale) → reshape → optional score_mod-via-narrow-and-cat → softmax → bmm_differentiable(weights, V) → reshape. All steps dispatch to native CUDA kernels: bmm goes through cuBLAS SgemmStridedBatched, softmax uses the GPU softmax kernel, cat uses the strided_cat kernel for score_mod reassembly.

Bonus correctness fix: the previous FlexAttentionBackward was a passthrough that returned grad_output as-is for Q/K/V (shape-wrong garbage). The new implementation has no custom backward — Q/K/V gradients now flow through the autograd chain of differentiable ops automatically, producing mathematically correct gradients for any score_mod whose ops are themselves differentiable (additive, multiplicative, or non-linear).

API compatibility: kept the per-(b,h) score_mod callback signature. When score_mod is provided, we extract per-(b,h) [n_q, n_k] sub-tensors via narrow().squeeze() (zero-copy stride views), call user code, lift back to [1,1,n_q,n_k] via unsqueeze, and reassemble with two cat() calls (heads then batches). Everything stays on the input device.

Tests: 8 total (3 new):
- test_flex_attention_numerical_value: hand-calculated reference output for B=H=1, n_q=n_k=d=d_v=2 case
- test_flex_attention_score_mod_additive_bias: verifies softmax-invariant constant bias produces identical output (strong correctness check on the narrow/cat reassembly path)
- test_flex_attention_grad_propagates_to_qkv: runs backward and verifies gradients land on Q, K, V with correct shapes and non-zero values (the bug the old passthrough couldn't catch)

All 860 ferrotorch-core lib tests pass. No new warnings.

Note: bmm() internally calls .contiguous() on permuted inputs, which currently still goes through CPU on strided CUDA tensors (#496). On contiguous Q/K/V inputs (the common case), the entire path is on-device.
